questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
What are the permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation?
What are the penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations?
questions.answer: Any person violating any provision of this chapter shall be guilty of a violation and punished by a fine not to exceed $250. Each day constitutes a separate offense, punishable by the same fine.

questions.question: Are there any exceptions for school/government/commercial properties?
questions.answer: Yes, there are exceptions for sounds connected with sporting events of public or private schools, sounds created by governmental agencies for public warning, and sounds by public utilities during their operations.

questions.question: How are violations reported and enforced?
questions.answer: Violations can be reported through sworn affidavits from two or more unrelated persons who agree on the times and durations of the noise, direct observation by an enforcement officer, or measuring noise levels with a sound-level device.

questions.question: Does the ordinance mention the benefits of leaving leaf debris and/or leaf mulching?
Does the ordinance require a report to be presented to the public?
